note_data.title: One-Act Plays — Part II
keyPoints: One-act plays are complete dramatic works performed in a single act without intervals or scene changes
They focus on a single incident, situation, or character development within a limited timeframe
Unity of time, place, and action is strictly maintained in one-act plays
Character development is rapid and concentrated, revealing personality through dialogue and action
The climax occurs quickly due to the compressed nature of the form
Themes are usually simple and direct, avoiding complex sub-plots
Stage settings are minimal and realistic to maintain focus on the central conflict
One-act plays originated as a reaction against lengthy three or five-act dramas

definitions.definition: A complete dramatic composition that is performed continuously without intervals, presenting a single situation or incident with unity of time, place and action.
The principle that a play should have one main plot without any sub-plots that might distract attention from the central theme.
The dramatic principle that the action of a play should occur within a single day or the actual time of performance.
The dramatic principle that the action should occur in a single location throughout the play.
The highest point of dramatic tension in a play where the main conflict reaches its peak and the turning point occurs.

importantQuestions.question: What are the characteristics of a one-act play?
importantQuestions.answer: One-act plays have unity of time, place and action, focus on single incident, rapid character development, simple themes, and minimal stage settings.

importantQuestions.question: Write a detailed note on the structure and features of one-act plays.
importantQuestions.answer: One-act plays are complete dramatic works performed without intervals. They maintain strict unity of time, place and action. The structure includes rapid exposition, quick character development, immediate conflict presentation, swift climax, and prompt resolution. Features include: single setting, limited characters, concentrated dialogue, simple themes, realistic situations, and economical use of dramatic devices. They avoid sub-plots and focus entirely on the central conflict or situation.

commonMistakes: Students confuse one-act plays with short stories - remember these are dramatic works for stage performance
Many forget to mention all three unities (time, place, action) when describing characteristics
Students often write about sub-plots when one-act plays specifically avoid them
Confusing climax with resolution - climax is the peak tension point, resolution follows it
Writing lengthy answers for short questions about simple definitions
examTips: This chapter usually appears as 1 MCQ and 1 short question worth 3-5 marks in BSEK papers
Always mention the three unities when discussing one-act play characteristics
Questions about 'features' and 'characteristics' appear frequently in past papers
Learn the exact definitions word-for-word as they appear in STBB textbook
Practice writing concise answers for 'Write a note on one-act plays' questions
note_data.keyDiagram: No specific diagram required for this literary topic

BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q6 (6 marks) — required comparison between one-act play and full-length play with examples
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q3 (3 marks) — asked to write characteristics of one-act play structure
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q5 (5 marks) — demanded explanation of unity of time, place and action in one-act plays

workedExample.question: Define one-act play and explain any three characteristics of one-act plays. (5 marks)
steps: Step 1: Write the exact textbook definition of one-act play
Step 2: State three characteristics clearly with headings
Step 3: Provide brief explanation for each characteristic using textbook language
workedExample.answer: A one-act play is a dramatic work that is complete in itself and can be performed without intermission in a single sitting. Three characteristics: (1) Unity of Action - focuses on one main incident or situation without subplots. (2) Limited Characters - usually has 2-8 characters to maintain focus and clarity. (3) Single Setting - action takes place in one location to maintain unity of place.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct definition, 1 mark for each characteristic stated, 1 mark for explanations, 1 mark for clear presentation

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general drama definitions instead of specific one-act play definition from STBB textbook
Avoid mixing characteristics of full-length plays with one-act plays - stick to textbook distinctions only
Never write examples from novels or poems when asked about one-act plays - only use dramatic examples
note_data.quickRecap: One-act plays are complete dramatic works performed without intermission. Key features include unity of time, place and action, limited characters (2-8), single setting, and focus on one main incident. They differ from full-length plays by being shorter and more concentrated in scope.
